roles.name: Producer in the Booth
roles.description: The man at the desk — designs the shape of the record and watches over the arrangement from the first take to the final mix.

roles.name: Tour Manager
roles.description: Marshals the band, the gear, and the timeline — keeps the lads on the bus and on the schedule.

roles.name: Tape Operator
roles.description: Steward of the reels, the patch bay, and the session log — every layout, field and permission set in their proper bin.

roles.name: Studio Engineer
roles.description: Forges Apex, LWC and platform features at the console — the one who makes the desk sing.

roles.name: Head of A&R
roles.description: Captain over all studio craft — sets the standards and signs off the senior session work.

roles.name: Session Player — Strings
roles.description: Brought in for the tricky overdubs — practiced craft, plays whatever the song needs.

roles.name: Session Player — Brass
roles.description: Carries discovery and configuration across the take under the senior player's eye.

roles.name: Mastering Engineer
roles.description: Architect of the great spans between studio and the world beyond — cuts the lacquer that ships.

roles.name: Mixing Desk Operator
roles.description: Lays the stones of API, middleware and event flows — rides the faders under the Mastering Engineer's plan.

roles.name: Apprentice Engineer
roles.description: Learns the craft of integration — webhooks, batch, callouts and the patterns of error handling, all from the back of the control room.

roles.name: Quality Listener
roles.description: Tends the wounded takes — finds the maladies in the mix before they reach the pressing plant.

roles.name: Press Officer
roles.description: Lifts the banner for the single and the album — leads all marketing craft from press release to picture sleeve.

roles.name: Album Sleeve Designer
roles.description: Strategist of campaigns — sounds the visual call to gather and convert the punters.

roles.name: Promo Film Director
roles.description: Carries the campaign forward into the field — content, creative, and the daily promo shoot.

roles.name: Music Publisher
roles.description: Records what is needed and what is promised — translates between the band and the suits at the label.

roles.name: Studio Manager
roles.description: Senior smith at the console — owns the deepest builds and mentors the apprentice engineers.

roles.name: Roadie
roles.description: Hauls the gear, learns the rig — first rung on the studio ladder, eyes wide open.

_realmNotes: windowOffsetWeeks values are negative so every realm starts at least one month in the past — past weeks get Time Entries (Step 6), future weeks get Schedules. Workstreams are organised as parent scope items (each with 2-3 child sub-scopes) — the wizard inserts parents first, then children with Parent_WorkStream__c set. The 4 realms are album eras: early-years (1962-1964), mid-period (1965-1966), studio-era (1967-1968), late-years (1969-1970).

workstreams.name: Recording at Abbey Road Studio Two

children.name: A Hard Day's Night — Soundtrack Cut
More Popular Than Jesus Damage Control

realms.projectName: Studio Era — Sgt. Pepper to White Album (1967-1968)
Late Years — Abbey Road & Let It Be (1969-1970)

workstreams.name: Recording Abbey Road & Let It Be

children.name: Let It Be — Phil Spector Remix

early-years.name: Decca Says No
early-years.description: Discovery sign-off — Decca passes, Parlophone bites. The journey begins.

early-years.name: Love Me Do Released
early-years.description: Build kickoff: first single hits the shops, climbs to #17.

early-years.name: Please Please Me at #1
early-years.description: Mid-project gate: debut album holds the top spot for 30 weeks.

early-years.name: Royal Variety Performance
early-years.description: Phase invoice trigger: 'rattle your jewellery' — the establishment salutes.

early-years.name: Ed Sullivan — 73M Viewers
early-years.description: Go-Live: America falls. Beatlemania goes global.

early-years.name: A Hard Day's Night Premiere
early-years.description: Hyper-care close: film and soundtrack ship together, era closes triumphant.

mid-period.name: Help! Sessions Begin
mid-period.description: Project kickoff: soundtrack album commissioned, cameras roll in the Bahamas.

mid-period.name: Shea Stadium Sells Out
mid-period.description: Build phase: 55,000 fans, screams louder than the Vox amps.

mid-period.name: Rubber Soul Charts #1
mid-period.description: UAT gate: folk pivot lands, the album as art form begins.

mid-period.name: Revolver Released
mid-period.description: Go-Live: Tomorrow Never Knows ships, tape loops break the form.

mid-period.name: Candlestick Park Farewell
mid-period.description: Hyper-care close: last paid concert, the touring era ends.

mid-period.name: Final Tour Settlement
mid-period.description: Final invoice: Brian closes the tour books for good.

studio-era.name: Sgt. Pepper Concept Greenlit
studio-era.description: Discovery: the alter-ego band concept signed off, 700 hours booked at Studio Two.

studio-era.name: A Day in the Life Recorded
studio-era.description: Build: 40-piece orchestra in evening dress, the great crescendo committed to tape.

studio-era.name: Sgt. Pepper Released
studio-era.description: Solution review: the Summer of Love has its soundtrack.

studio-era.name: Apple Records Launches
studio-era.description: Go-Live: Saville Row opens, Hey Jude is the first single.

studio-era.name: White Album Released
studio-era.description: Project close: double LP ships, cracks in the band visible in the grooves.

studio-era.name: Apple Boutique Closes
studio-era.description: Final invoice: stock given away, the experiment in retail ends.

late-years.name: Twickenham Rehearsals Begin
late-years.description: Kickoff at the cold film stage — Get Back project conceived.

late-years.name: Rooftop Concert
late-years.description: Discovery: 42 minutes on the Saville Row roof, the last live performance.

late-years.name: Abbey Road Released
late-years.description: Build: side-two medley delivered, the final masterpiece pressed.

late-years.name: Paul Announces Departure
late-years.description: UAT acceptance: the press release confirms what everyone knew.

late-years.name: Let It Be Released
late-years.description: Go-Live + handoff: Phil Spector's mix ships, the band sails into the West.

late-years.name: Final Apple Settlement
late-years.description: Final invoice from Apple Corps — the partnership formally dissolves.

John Lennon.notes: Bag-In retreat at the Amsterdam Hilton with Yoko.

Paul McCartney.notes: High Park Farm in Kintyre — escaping the Apple drama.

George Harrison.notes: Trip to India for sitar lessons with Ravi Shankar.

Ringo Starr.notes: Drum kit refurbishment and family time in Surrey.

Brian Epstein.notes: NEMS Enterprises board day in Liverpool.

Geoff Emerick.notes: Burnout recovery — White Album sessions took a toll.

Neil Aspinall.notes: Magical Mystery Tour bus refit logistics.

Phil Spector.notes: Wall of Sound consultation in LA.
